summary: AS207B is the MINI CENSUS INTERROGATION FORM -- the compact companion to AS207A's full census grid. 28 cords, 1-PAIRE architecture depth L2, SINGLE CLUSTER, 3 colors (W 57%, YB 32%, KB 11%). 9 STRING (32 percent, 100 percent dict hit), 14 INT, 5 EMPTY. VOCABULARY: pi x4 (WHO?, FOUR interrogatives), cha x3 (na-suffix, grammatical obligation / compliance marker), pa x1 + wa x1 (1st-person-object 'me'). Same museum as AS207A (Museum of Science, Buffalo, NY) + same Ascher 1987 publication. SCALED-DOWN FORM: AS207A = 253 cords with 18 interrogatives (pi x9 + piqa x9); AS207B = 28 cords with 4 interrogatives (pi x4). Cord ratio ~9:1, interrogative ratio 4.5:1. If AS207A is the FULL CENSUS FORM (3x3 color-dimension grid + 9-answer register), AS207B is the SUMMARY COVER SHEET (4 essential questions without the full grid or answer register). Same scribal tradition, different levels of detail.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  28 cords. 9 STRING (32 percent, 100 percent dict hit), 14 INT, 5 EMPTY.
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. SINGLE CLUSTER.
  Colors: W(16, 57%), YB(9, 32%), KB(3, 11%). 3 colors.
  Provenance: UNKNOWN. Museum: Museum of Science, Buffalo, New York.
  Published: Ascher and Ascher (1987). Mathematics of the Incas, alongside AS207A.

VOCABULARY (9 STRING, 100 percent dict hit)
  pi  x4 -- WHO? (FOUR interrogatives -- the core of the form)
  cha x3 -- na-suffix (grammatical; on onset correction cha->na may indicate OBLIGATION / compliance marker)
  pa  x1 -- suffix or abbreviated form
  wa  x1 -- 1ST-PERSON-OBJECT 'me' / 'to me'

COMPANION TO AS207A
  AS207A (full census form): 253 cords, 18 interrogatives (pi x9 + piqa x9), 3x3 color-dimension
                              grid at cords 91-99, answer register at cords 216-240, 43 clusters.
  AS207B (this document): 28 cords, 4 interrogatives (pi x4), single cluster, 3 colors.
  CORD RATIO 9:1. INTERROGATIVE RATIO 4.5:1.
  AS207B is the COVER SHEET / SUMMARY -- four essential questions where AS207A asked eighteen.

STRUCTURE
  Single cluster, 28 cords.
  9 STRING cords interspersed with 14 INT + 5 EMPTY.
  pi x4 = the core interrogative function distributed across the cluster.
  cha x3 = administrative-grammatical marker (obligation / compliance under cha->na onset correction).

HYPOTHESIS
  AS207B is the summary or cover-sheet version of AS207A's full census form. Four essential questions
  are asked without the 3x3 color-dimension grid elaboration and without the 9-answer register. The
  ratio (AS207B at 1/9 the size of AS207A, 4/18 the interrogatives) suggests AS207B is handed to the
  administrator who does not need every detail, only the 4 essential questions. Same scribal
  tradition, different levels of administrative detail.
narrative:
Who? Four times, on a khipu half the size of its companion.

AS207B is the cover sheet. Where AS207A asked eighteen questions across a three-by-three colour grid and registered nine identified persons as answers, AS207B asks four. Twenty-eight cords against two hundred and fifty-three. One cluster against forty-three. Three colours against seven. Everything is scaled down, compressed, essential.

The full form at AS207A interrogates in three dimensions: who in this category, who with these goods, who in this data, each question asked three times in its proper colour. That document is designed for the clerk doing the census, working through the grid systematically, generating nine specific names to enter on the answer register further down the cord.

The cover sheet at AS207B asks pi four times and moves on. Who. Who. Who. Who. Four base questions without colour-dimension elaboration, without a 3x3 grid structure, without a full answer register. The document you hand to the administrator who needs to know the headline count, the four essential identifications, without every supporting dimension.

Alongside the four pi cords, three cha and a pa and a wa. Cha three times carries the administrative grammatical marker -- possibly under the cha-to-na onset correction, a compliance or obligation marker. Pa is a suffix or abbreviated form. Wa is the first-person object pronoun -- 'to me,' 'me-as-target,' the administrator identifying himself as the recipient of the census-reply.

Same museum as AS207A. Same catalogue in Buffalo. Same Ascher 1987 publication. The two forms were collected and published as a pair, and as a pair they make sense: one the full questionnaire, one the summary.

prior_work.claim: AS207B was published alongside AS207A by Ascher and Ascher (1987) in Mathematics of the Incas; the two documents share museum catalogue at the Museum of Science, Buffalo, NY.
prior_work.citation: Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1987). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu. Dover Publications.

prior_work.claim: The cha->na onset correction is a documented ALBA syllabary convention for interpreting chi/cha onsets whose base form exists in the dictionary; the correction applies to census-compliance vocabulary at least.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). ALBA syllabary v3 onset conventions.

prior_work.claim: Inca administrative practice produced multiple levels of census documentation: full-detail registers, summary tables, and cover sheets at different bureaucratic levels (Murra 1980).
prior_work.citation: Murra, J. V. (1980). The Economic Organization of the Inka State; Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu.

v3_contribution.claim: COMPANION IDENTIFICATION: AS207B as SCALED-DOWN summary of AS207A. Cord ratio 9:1, interrogative ratio 4.5:1. Same scribal tradition, different bureaucratic detail-levels. First documented corpus instance of a full-form + cover-sheet pair.
32 PERCENT STRING DENSITY on a 28-cord document: among the highest text-density ratios at small scale. Consistent with a COMPACT-FORM design prioritising essential questions over detailed data.
1ST-PERSON-RECIPIENT MARKER wa: the 1OBJ 'me' at one cord identifies the administrator as the recipient of the census-reply. Rare explicit first-person marker in an administrative document.
SINGLE-CLUSTER COMPACT FORM: unlike AS207A's 43 clusters, AS207B is a SINGLE-CLUSTER document. The structural compression fits the summary / cover-sheet function.

companions.note: FULL CENSUS FORM companion (253 cords, 18 interrogatives, 3x3 grid, 9-answer register). AS207B is the SCALED-DOWN summary / cover sheet. Same museum, same Ascher 1987 publication. The pair makes sense only together.

evidence.test: Buffalo Museum of Science + Ascher 1987 co-publication + structural compression

companions.note: Central-coast census register (kiki x11). Different census sub-type (enumeration vs interrogation) but same census-class function.
Lineage register (14 kaka roll call). Census sub-type peer.

Compressed juridical (28 cords, pi x2 interrogatives). Exactly the same cord count as AS207B (28) and uses pi interrogatives at a different function. Two 28-cord compact Inca administrative documents with interrogatives.
Pachacamac oracle khipu with concentrated interrogatives. AS207B's compact interrogative form parallels oracle-interrogation conventions.
MNAAHP Lima identity register (32 cords, chiki x6). Similar small-scale administrative document with identity-focused vocabulary.

word_reading.note: 9 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. pi, cha, pa, wa all dictionary-stable. The cha->na onset correction for cha x3 is principled but its specific semantic load (obligation vs compliance vs formal-administrative) is interpretive.

document_type.note: census_interrogation sub-type supported by 4-pi core + single-cluster structure + AS207A structural compression parallel. Smaller sample than AS207A; classification relies on the pair-relationship with the full-form companion.

narrative.note: The 'summary / cover-sheet of AS207A full form' reading is internally coherent and structurally anchored on the 9:1 cord ratio and 4:18 interrogative ratio. The specific interpretation of cha as obligation-marker is interpretive; the core 4-pi interrogation is directly observable.

data_integrity.note: 100 percent dict hit. Small 28-cord scale limits cross-checks. 5 empty cords structural. No Ascher layer summarised. The AS207A companion validates the interrogation-form reading.

sections.title: Single-Cluster Compact Form
sections.reading: Entire document is a single cluster of 28 cords. pi x4 (interrogatives) distributed across the cluster + cha x3 (grammatical / compliance) + pa + wa (1st-person-object 'me'). The compact interrogation addresses four essential questions to the administrator-recipient.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Census interrogation companion analysis -- AS207A + AS207B pair.
Sivan, J. (2026). ALBA syllabary v3 onset conventions -- cha->na correction.
Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1987). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu.
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- pi interrogative; 1OBJ wa.
Murra, J. V. (1980). The Economic Organization of the Inka State.
Museum of Science, Buffalo, New York -- AS207B catalogue record.

Compare AS207A (full census interrogation companion), UR037 + AS026B (other census conventions), AS030 (28-cord compressed juridical with pi), UR193 (oracle interrogatives), HP051 (compact identity register).
